text:
EMPLOYMENT AGREEMENT
Position
The Employee shall serve as Software Engineer.
Salary
Monthly salary shall be INR 60,000 payable on the last working day.
Probation
Probation period shall be 6 months.
Termination
Either party may terminate by giving 30 days written notice.
Confidentiality
Employee shall keep company information confidential.
Non-Compete
Employee shall not work for a direct competitor for 12 months after leaving.
Governing Law
This agreement shall be governed by the laws of India.

summary.one_line_summary: Moderate-risk Employment Contract with 1 clauses. Minor tweaks are suggested for the 0 flagged areas.
summary.ai_executive_summary: This employment contract contains 1 clauses. No high or critical risk clauses were flagged inside the document. 8 recommended legal protections are missing, including Force Majeure Clause, and Dispute Resolution Clause. Overall risk score: 37/100. Legal recommendation: Sign After Negotiation.
summary.overall_recommendation: Sign After Negotiation. The contract contains standard terms but has moderate risk areas that should be adjusted.

top_risks.reason: Missing Force Majeure clause. Under extreme circumstances (natural disasters, pandemics), the employer could terminate employment instantly or withhold pay without a defined legal framework.
top_risks.consequences: Add: 'Performance of duties and payment of salary may be modified or suspended mutually in the event of continuous force majeure occurrences.'

top_risks.reason: Without a dispute resolution process, internal issues (appraisals, harassment, contract breaches) may result in immediate litigation or public labor disputes rather than confidential mediation.
top_risks.consequences: Add: 'Any dispute arising under this contract shall first be referred to the internal HR Grievance Committee, followed by mutual mediation before legal action.'

top_risks.reason: No confidentiality protection is present. The employee can share sensitive trade secrets, source code, customer lists, and business strategies with competitors without violating the contract.
top_risks.consequences: Add: 'The Employee shall not disclose any confidential information, trade secrets, or client data of the Employer during or after the term of employment.'

top_risks.reason: No exit notice period is defined. The employee can walk out on a critical project with zero warning, or the employer can fire the employee instantly without severance.
top_risks.consequences: Add: 'Either party may terminate employment by giving 30 days' written notice or salary in lieu of notice.'

top_risks.reason: For fixed-term contracts, the lack of renewal terms means employment simply lapses, creating job insecurity for the employee and operational disruptions for the employer.
top_risks.consequences: Add: 'This contract may be extended or renewed upon mutual written agreement of both parties at least 15 days prior to expiry.'

clauses.title: Governing Law
clauses.text:
EMPLOYMENT AGREEMENT
Position
The Employee shall serve as Software Engineer.
Salary
Monthly salary shall be INR 60,000 payable on the last working day.
Probation
Probation period shall be 6 months.
Termination
Either party may terminate by giving 30 days written notice.
Confidentiality
Employee shall keep company information confidential.
Non-Compete
Employee shall not work for a direct competitor for 12 months after leaving.
Governing Law
This agreement shall be governed by the laws of India.
clauses.reason: No issues identified. Clause conforms to standard legal templates.
clauses.explanation: Vague or foreign governing laws create extreme uncertainty and require foreign legal experts to resolve disputes, adding immense overhead.
clauses.rewrite:
EMPLOYMENT AGREEMENT
Position
The Employee shall serve as Software Engineer.
Salary
Monthly salary shall be INR 60,000 payable on the last working day.
Probation
Probation period shall be 6 months.
Termination
Either party may terminate by giving 30 days written notice.
Confidentiality
Employee shall keep company information confidential.
Non-Compete
Employee shall not work for a direct competitor for 12 months after leaving.
Governing Law
This agreement shall be governed by the laws of India.
clauses.negotiation_advice: Ensure the governing law is specified as Indian law, particularly for domestic transactions. Foreign governing laws are costly to interpret and contest.

missing_protections.name: Force Majeure Clause
missing_protections.why_missing: Missing Force Majeure clause. Under extreme circumstances (natural disasters, pandemics), the employer could terminate employment instantly or withhold pay without a defined legal framework.
missing_protections.remedy: Add: 'Performance of duties and payment of salary may be modified or suspended mutually in the event of continuous force majeure occurrences.'

missing_protections.name: Dispute Resolution Clause
missing_protections.why_missing: Without a dispute resolution process, internal issues (appraisals, harassment, contract breaches) may result in immediate litigation or public labor disputes rather than confidential mediation.
missing_protections.remedy: Add: 'Any dispute arising under this contract shall first be referred to the internal HR Grievance Committee, followed by mutual mediation before legal action.'

missing_protections.name: Confidentiality Clause
missing_protections.why_missing: No confidentiality protection is present. The employee can share sensitive trade secrets, source code, customer lists, and business strategies with competitors without violating the contract.
missing_protections.remedy: Add: 'The Employee shall not disclose any confidential information, trade secrets, or client data of the Employer during or after the term of employment.'

missing_protections.name: Notice Period Clause
missing_protections.why_missing: No exit notice period is defined. The employee can walk out on a critical project with zero warning, or the employer can fire the employee instantly without severance.
missing_protections.remedy: Add: 'Either party may terminate employment by giving 30 days' written notice or salary in lieu of notice.'

missing_protections.name: Renewal Option Clause
missing_protections.why_missing: For fixed-term contracts, the lack of renewal terms means employment simply lapses, creating job insecurity for the employee and operational disruptions for the employer.
missing_protections.remedy: Add: 'This contract may be extended or renewed upon mutual written agreement of both parties at least 15 days prior to expiry.'

missing_protections.name: Refund / Compensation Clause
missing_protections.why_missing: Missing clear terms on final settlement timelines, potentially leading to the employer delaying severance, unpaid bonuses, or expense refunds post-termination.
missing_protections.remedy: Add: 'Upon termination, the Employer shall release all due salary, gratuity, and expense refunds within 15 days of the employee's final working day.'

missing_protections.name: Employee Indemnification Clause
missing_protections.why_missing: Without an indemnity limit, the employee could be held personally liable for company losses or third-party lawsuits arising from standard business decisions.
missing_protections.remedy: Add: 'The Employer shall defend and indemnify the Employee against any third-party claims or liabilities arising out of the performance of their standard duties.'

missing_protections.name: Limitation of Employee Liability
missing_protections.why_missing: No liability cap protects the employee. The company could sue the employee for unlimited damages if a coding bug or business mistake causes financial loss.
missing_protections.remedy: Add: 'Except in cases of fraud or willful misconduct, the Employee's liability for any loss caused to the Employer shall be capped at a maximum of 3 months' basic salary.'
